name: sidecar
description: Claude Code plugin pack with strict concept separation. Top-level dirs: hooks/ (PreToolUse·SessionStart auto-behavior), commands/ (/slash-command invoked), skills/ (Skill tool invocable). 1 plugin = 1 concept; no mixing.

plugins.name: commons
plugins.description: UserPromptSubmit + SessionStart + PreCompact + PostCompact hook — injects a cross-project do/dont layer from commons.tape (granular `@D g0..g72` form, no tool/usage) + a live `pool` roster snapshot (~/.pool/pool.json) appended per injection so the host lineup is visible without running `pool list`; UserPromptSubmit re-injects every turn so the layer never fades; SessionStart additionally appends COMMANDS.md (the cross-project slash-command catalog) once per session. Carries LATTICE_POLICY.md (real-limits-first SSOT).

plugins.name: prefs
plugins.description: /prefs — view or set language preferences (3 axes: code authoring · doc authoring · response language to user). /prefs show · /prefs code <lang> · /prefs docs <lang> · /prefs response <lang>. Writes the FIXED SSOT ~/.claude/plugins/data/prefs-sidecar/prefs.json (defaults code=english · docs=english · response=korean) via `_prefs.hexa` (shipped in `prefs-hook`). The `prefs-hook` plugin reads the same SSOT and auto-injects current values every turn. Command half of the prefs split.

plugins.name: prefs-hook
plugins.description: UserPromptSubmit + SessionStart + PreCompact + PostCompact hook — injects the user's current language preferences (code · docs · response axes) as additionalContext from the FIXED SSOT ~/.claude/plugins/data/prefs-sidecar/prefs.json (written by the `prefs` command plugin); re-injects every turn so the preference never fades from context. Type-gated on read (a non-string field falls back to the default). hexa-lang (`_prefs.hexa inject`, via `hexa run`). Hook half of the prefs split. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: hexa-lsp
plugins.description: Wire the hexa-lang LSP server (`hexa lsp`) for `.hexa` files. LSP config in plugin-root `.lsp.json` (canonical Claude Code filename, dot-prefixed). Minimal: only `.hexa` claimed.

plugins.name: tape-lsp
plugins.description: Wire `tape-lsp` (canonical .tape v1.2 LSP — diagnostics + hover) for `.tape` files. LSP config in plugin-root `.lsp.json` (canonical Claude Code filename, dot-prefixed). Requires `tape-lsp` on PATH (install via `hx install dancinlab/tape`).

plugins.name: kosmos-lsp
plugins.description: Wire `kosmos-lsp` (kosmos/1.1 multimodal knowledge-anchor manifest LSP — diagnostics + hover, re-validated on every edit) for `.kosmos` files. LSP config in plugin-root `.lsp.json` (canonical Claude Code filename, dot-prefixed). Requires `kosmos-lsp` on PATH (install via `hx install dancinlab/kosmos`).

plugins.name: n6-lsp
plugins.description: Wire `n6-lsp` (NEXUS-6 knowledge-atlas grammar LSP — diagnostics + hover, re-validated on every edit) for `.n6` files. LSP config in plugin-root `.lsp.json` (canonical Claude Code filename, dot-prefixed). Requires `n6-lsp` on PATH (install via `hx install dancinlab/n6`).

plugins.name: hxc-lsp
plugins.description: Wire `hxc-lsp` (HXC hexa-canonical wire/storage format LSP — diagnostics + hover, re-validated on every edit) for `.hxc` files. LSP config in plugin-root `.lsp.json` (canonical Claude Code filename, dot-prefixed). Requires `hxc-lsp` on PATH (install via `hx install dancinlab/hxc`).

plugins.name: project-tape
plugins.description: PreCompact + PostCompact hook — re-injects <project-root>/project.tape as additionalContext so the project's identity + governance survive auto-compaction. No-op when project.tape is absent.

plugins.name: git-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) git-push safety guard, in hexa-lang (`_git_guard.hexa`, via `hexa run`). DENIES force-type push — `git push --force` / `-f` · `--force-with-lease` · `git push <remote> +<refspec>` (refspec-level force); quotes stripped before tokenizing (0.4.2) so `'--force-with-lease=x'` / `+"refspec"` can't slip past. STALE-BASE advisory (0.5.0, non-blocking): for a non-force push, emits additionalContext when the current branch trails the remote default base (origin/HEAD, else origin/main) by >= 20 commits — the shared-workdir branch-swap / silent-revert fingerprint; push still proceeds. Hook-bypass (--no-verify) NOT blocked (left to user discipline). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: workdir-guard
plugins.description: SessionStart advisory (hexa-lang `_workdir_guard.hexa`, via `hexa run`) that fires once per session, and only when the current git working tree is shared by >= 2 concurrent `claude` agents. Detection (~0.1s): `ps` lists claude PIDs (comm == 'claude' — lsof's command field shows the version string, not 'claude'), then one `lsof -a -p <list> -d cwd` collects their cwds, awk prefix-matched against `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`. A shared working tree = shared `.git/index` + `HEAD`, so a sibling agent can swap HEAD out from under you, commingle your staged files, or leave your push on a stale branch. Injects a one-shot reminder to do git landings in an isolated worktree and never reset/switch/delete another agent's active branch. Grouped by working tree, not repo — silent in solo sessions (count 1) and self-extinguishes once you move into a worktree. Advisory-only, non-blocking. Proactive complement to git-guard's reactive stale-base push advisory. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: worktree-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory for `git worktree add`, in hexa-lang (`_worktree_guard.hexa`, via `hexa run`). When a Bash command creates a git worktree, emits non-blocking additionalContext with the durable-worktree drill: in a heavily-concurrent or synced repo an isolated worktree dir can be removed out from under you (a sibling agent's `git worktree prune`, a workspace sync, or the macOS /tmp reaper) and any UNCOMMITTED edits vanish with it — so commit + push promptly after editing, don't hold uncommitted work across turns, land via PR then `git worktree remove` (verification builds AFTER the commit, so a failed build never costs the work). Stronger note when the target path is under a volatile location (/tmp · /private/tmp · /var/folders). Advisory-only (guards-narrow-scope: non-blocking additionalContext > deny for hygiene). Sister of workdir-guard (shared-tree SessionStart advisory) + worktree-gc (merged-worktree prune). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: memory-lint
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory for the auto-memory index file (`memory/MEMORY.md`), implemented in hexa-lang (`_memory_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Reads the post-write file when tool_input.file_path ends in `memory/MEMORY.md` and emits non-blocking additionalContext for: entry count > 30 (hot-tier soft cap → migrate older items to sibling MEMORY.archive.md) · any non-comment line > 150 chars (TLDR refactor budget — verb + nouns, push trailing clauses into the sibling `*.md` file). Keeps the always-loaded MEMORY.md dense + greppable as memories accumulate. Advisory-only by design (per guards-narrow-scope: non-blocking additionalContext > deny for hygiene).

plugins.name: drift-guard
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory that prevents design-drift regression — when a design-defining surface is edited but MEMORY.md + CLAUDE.md/project.tape are not, the next session (which loads memory at startup, not the diff) regresses to the old design. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_drift_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Fires on any of: (1) SSOT file edit — path ends in `.tape` or `/CLAUDE.md`; (2) a new artifact under a `decisions/` folder; (3) an explicit `@design-change` marker in the post-write content. Skips the mirror targets themselves (`/memory/`, MEMORY.md) and the guard's own install dir. Emits non-blocking additionalContext nudging the model to mirror the change into the project auto-memory (memory file + MEMORY.md pointer), or — for a cross-project governance rule — to ask the user to `sidecar sign` commons.tape/project.tape. De-dupes once per (session, file) via a CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA marker. Advisory-only. 0.1.1 — kills self-trigger noise: sentinel scan now limited to source-code extensions (.hexa/.py/.sh/…), so prose-heavy docs (marketplace/README/CHANGELOG/SKILL) that DOCUMENT the marker no longer self-trigger on every edit; sidecar repo's own /hooks/drift-guard/ tree is now skipped (in addition to CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT cache). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: inbox-log-lint
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory for the INBOX domain log (`INBOX.log.md`). Fires when tool_input.file_path ends in `INBOX.log.md` and reads the post-write file to emit non-blocking additionalContext when the cross-project handoff log piles up: entry count (`## ` headers) > 20 (soft cap) · fully-resolved entries (>=1 `- [x]`, zero `- [ ]`) > 12 · non-blank lines > 250 — each suggests moving closed handoffs to a sibling `INBOX.archive.log.md` so the active log stays open + recent. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_inbox_log_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Advisory-only by design (per the guards-narrow-scope policy: non-blocking additionalContext > deny for hygiene). Sister of memory-lint (same pattern for memory/MEMORY.md).

plugins.name: sidecar-lint
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) auto-lint that fires on `git commit` in any Claude Code marketplace plugin pack (any repo with .claude-plugin/marketplace.json), implemented in hexa-lang (`_sidecar_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Non-blocking findings as additionalContext: stale-history patterns in staged diff (commons @D g15) · hardcoded /Users//home/ absolute paths in staged diff (commons @D g13 · sidecar @D s3) · marketplace.json plugin entry ↔ each plugin.json version drift (commons @D g22) · hook .sh missing user-exec bit · missing CHANGELOG.md at repo root (commons @D g29) · (when .claude-plugin/profiles.json is present) any marketplace plugin missing a tier there (sidecar @D s7 — untagged silently defaults to 'personal', hidden from the minimal/hexa profiles) · any registered MCP-server plugin (project.tape @D s15 — `mcps/` source or `mcpServers` in plugin.json; no MCP-server plugins, stdio handshake blocks sessions). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: hexa-native
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) hard block for `.py` / `.sh` writes inside any project rooted at a directory containing a `project.tape` marker (sidecar's canonical project identity file), implemented in hexa-lang (`_hexa_native.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Covers BOTH write channels: structured file tools (Write/Edit/NotebookEdit) and the Bash write channels — `> X.py`, `>> X.sh`, heredoc-to-redirect (`cat > X.py << EOF`), `tee [-a] X.py`, plus non-redirect channels `dd of=X.py` and `cp`/`mv` destination (0.3.2); relative targets resolved against the payload `cwd`. Quote-aware redirects; fd-dup (`2>&1`) yields no target; non-write Bash (`python X.py`, `cat X.py`, `chmod X.sh`) passes through. Unbounded channels (`python -c open(...)`, `sed -i`, `perl -e`, `install -t`) are NOT covered — this narrows the common write paths, not a sandbox. Walks up parent dirs of the target; if a `project.tape` is found, the write is denied with an action-first message that leads with the fix (re-issue as `.hexa`) and demotes the refusal reason, so the reader reroutes instead of reading “blocked” and punting to a doc / the user. NO opt-out: no env var, no config file, no exception list, no self-exclusion — hard-coded by design. Targets only `.py` / `.sh`; other extensions pass through. Projects without `project.tape` are unaffected.

plugins.name: sign-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) sign-gate for governance-SSOT files, implemented in hexa-lang (`_sign_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). A small registry (GATED) of high-stakes files — currently `commons.tape` (cross-project do/dont layer), `project.tape` (per-project identity + governance), and `.gitignore` (commit-hygiene: silently controls what enters version control, so a stealth agent edit could un-ignore a secret or hide a file from review) — may be edited by the agent ONLY while a fresh user sign-off token exists. The user mints one with `sidecar sign <key>` (`commons` · `project` · `gitignore`), writing the current epoch to `~/.sidecar/signs/<key>.sign`; a token is valid for 300s (5 min). Covers both write channels (mirrors hexa-native): structured file tools and the Bash write channels (`> X`, `>> X`, heredoc, `tee X`, `dd of=X`, `cp`/`mv` dest; relative targets resolved against `cwd`). Writes buried inside an invoked CLI (e.g. `sidecar init`) are not visible at the top-level command string, so the official scaffolder is unaffected — only the agent's own direct writes gate. The hook CODE-ENFORCES the no-self-mint rule: it hard-denies the agent's mint command `sidecar sign <key>` (list / clear stay allowed) AND any write forging a token under `~/.sidecar/signs/`. Because the agent's tool calls (Bash · Write/Edit) route through this hook, the agent CANNOT self-mint. Mint detection (0.1.5) is narrowed to a real command invocation: the Bash channel is scanned on a quote-masked copy and sidecar must sit at a command position (first token or right after a shell separator), so a sidecar sign <key> MENTION inside a quoted commit message or PR body is no longer mis-flagged - pre-0.1.5 it wrongly blocked legit commits that referenced the command. (The interactive `!` bang a USER types in the TUI prompt does NOT route through the hook and mints freely — the intended user path, safe because the agent cannot emit a `!` bang.) `bin/sidecar` carries an OPT-IN /dev/tty second layer (`SIDECAR_SIGN_REQUIRE_TTY=1`) defeating hook-parser-evading obfuscation. The fresh token is the sole precondition (project.tape @D s11: gate on a real precondition, not an opt-out switch); enforcement is the hard deny (project.tape @D s13). To gate another file: add one row to GATED and reship. NO env-var / flag override by design.

plugins.name: plist-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit) hard block for `.plist` writes, implemented in hexa-lang (`_plist_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Any target file ending in `.plist` (LaunchAgents · LaunchDaemons · Info.plist · UserDefaults) is denied with a redirecting message — the agent cannot distinguish user-explicit from model-autonomous intent, so all .plist writes are refused globally; if the user truly wants one, they run the command themselves outside the agent path. LaunchAgents / LaunchDaemons are persistence surfaces (auto-launch on login / boot), so silent writes risk installing daemons the user did not ask for. Enforces commons @D g37 (plist user-request only). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: cloud-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw rented-GPU pod dispatch (commons @D g8). Four orthogonal detectors run on every Bash command: (a) pod-CLI + remote-verb pair scan anywhere in the command — runpodctl {exec|ssh|send|receive|send-file|receive-file|send-file-to-pod|receive-file-from-pod|port} · vast(ai) {exec|ssh|scp|copy|attach-ssh|execute}; (b) ssh/scp/rsync/sftp against `*.runpod.io` / `proxy.runpod.net` / `*.vast.ai` hosts; (c) curl/wget/http(ie)/xh against `api.runpod.io` / `api.runpod.ai` / `rest.runpod.io` / `runpod.io/graphql` / `console.vast.ai` / `vast.ai/api` URLs; (d) dispatcher-script content scan — `bash/sh/zsh/dash <path>` reads the script and re-runs detectors (a)/(b)/(c) on its body, closing the workaround of pre-writing a dispatch.sh with raw `runpodctl ssh` and invoking it. Shell separators normalized to whitespace before tokenizing — `cd /x && runpodctl ssh …`, `bash -c '… vast exec …'`, env-prefixes all caught. Pod-lifecycle verbs (create / get / start / stop / remove / show / search / launch / destroy / config / project / version) pass through. Redirects to `hexa cloud {run|nohup|poll|copy-to|copy-from|copy-dir-*|preflight}`. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_cloud_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pod-monitor
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for GPU pod fires (`hexa cloud nohup` / `hexa cloud fire` / `hexa cloud run`). Emits non-blocking additionalContext reminding the agent of commons @D g57 — three invariants: set `SAVE_POD=1` envar (pod retain when SSH drops), attach the 🛰️ Monitor tool to the streaming log (no `tail -f` / sleep-poll), AND ⏱ estimate walltime up front with pod-hours headroom (undersize → pod auto-killed mid-run → result LOST). Closes the R8a-LOST class. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_pod_monitor.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). 0.1.3 — `_has_pod_fire` uses token-position match past env-var prefix (the cloud verb must SIT at command position, not be a substring of the cmd string) so commit messages / grep patterns / docs that merely mention the phrase no longer trigger noise. `fire` verb (hexa-lang PR #1309) added. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: monitor-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for background / long-running shell launches (trailing `&` · `nohup`/`setsid`/`disown` · the Bash tool's `run_in_background` flag). When the rate-limit-survivable pattern is incomplete, emits non-blocking additionalContext enforcing commons @D g10 — the while-monitor pattern: (1) detach the job (`nohup`/`setsid`) so a session rate-limit death does NOT SIGHUP it, (2) write a progress log (`> /tmp/<job>.log 2>&1`) as a durable record + Monitor re-attach point, (3) attach the 🛰️ Monitor tool to the LOG (not the live pipe — token-light, re-attachable after a rate-limit), with an OS-side `while` heartbeat for liveness (no model-side sleep-poll). Stays silent when the job is already detached AND logged. Closes the rate-limit-force-kill class for general local background work; general-local sister of pod-monitor (GPU pods) and proactive sister of limit-guard (post-hoc checkpoint). Implemented in hexa-lang (`_monitor_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). 0.1.1 — `hexa cloud nohup`/`hexa cloud fire` patterns ALWAYS fire a cloud-bridge hint (basic detach+log is inherent to the verb, but the cloud workflow has a separate requirement: thread the logfile path from nohup into `hexa cloud tail` so Monitor can attach). 0.1.2 — the `hexa cloud fire` verb + `__MONITOR_HANDLE__={…}` JSON-line contract are now SHIPPED upstream (hexa-lang PR #1306 + #1309); cloud-bridge hint points to the live atomic workflow (`hexa cloud fire <host> -- <argv>` → grep handle line → attach Monitor to `tail_cmd`) instead of the pre-ship 3-step manual handoff. 0.1.3 — cloud-bridge trigger now uses token-position match past env-var prefix (`_is_cloud_pod`) instead of `cmd.contains(...)` substring — kills the noise where commit messages / grep patterns merely mentioning the phrase fired the advisory. Also fires the hint even when `is_bg` would short-circuit (cloud fire/nohup are inherently dispatch verbs). 0.1.4 — `has_detach` follows the same token-position pattern via `_has_detach_pos`. 0.1.5 — `has_log` also via `_has_log_pos` + `_redirect_targets` (quote-aware extraction of `>`/`>>` redirect targets, `tee` invocations with file args, `<<` heredocs) — commands that merely MENTION `.log` in an unrelated arg (e.g. `grep nohup /var/log/foo.log`) no longer satisfy the log-sink check incorrectly. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: throttle-guard
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Task|Agent) cross-session coordinator for the TRANSIENT server throttle — 'Server is temporarily limiting requests (not your usage limit) · Rate limited' — that fires when too many concurrent requests hit at once (several sessions, or a wide fan-out). Distinct from limit-guard (the 5h/7d USAGE/session cap → checkpoint + RESUME). On detect: (1) writes/extends a SHARED cooldown marker ~/.sidecar/throttle.json with an exponential backoff (15·30·60·120·240·300s, capped, 120s storm window) so every session/round reads ONE backoff window — killing the thundering-herd re-fire where all sessions retry in lockstep and re-trip the throttle; (2) injects a non-blocking directive — back off with JITTER (never retry in lockstep), cut fan-out WIDTH to ≤1 until the window passes, resume by re-reading not re-running. Concurrency control that RESPECTS the throttle (clears once in-flight load drops), not evasion. Gated to NOT double-fire with limit-guard (skips when the result names a usage/session limit). Implemented in hexa-lang (`_throttle_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: ai-api-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw AI-API calls when a sidecar CLI wraps the same operation, implemented in hexa-lang (`_ai_api_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Two deny patterns: (1) `curl`/`wget`/`http`/`httpie` to an AI hostname (`api.openai.com` · `api.anthropic.com` · `fal.run` · `api.replicate.com` · `generativelanguage.googleapis.com`); (2) inline `python(3)?` / `node` / `deno` / `bun` with `-c`/`-e` AND `import` of `openai` / `anthropic` / `fal_client` / `replicate` / `google.generativeai`. Redirects to `/imagine` (image) / `/research` (text) / etc. — commons @D g50 enforcement. Whitespace/line-continuation is normalized before matching (0.1.3) so multi-space / split forms still match; dynamic imports (`exec(...)`, base64) remain out of scope (static scan limit). General SDK usage in committed code files (no inline `-c`/`-e`) passes through. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: verify-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw verification-tool usage cited as primary evidence, implemented in hexa-lang (`_verify_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Denies first-token ∈ {wolframscript, mathematica, WolframKernel}, and inline-Python verification via `python(3)? -c '...'` containing `import sympy` / `from sympy` / `import pyphi` / `import wolframclient` with a redirecting message pointing to `hexa verify` (commons @D g5). General sympy / wolframclient usage in committed code files passes through — only inline `-c` verification probes (and the standalone Wolfram CLIs) are refused. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pr-cycle
plugins.description: /pr-cycle — one-shot PR cycle command. Pushes the current branch (`git push -u origin HEAD`) then `gh pr create --fill`; the `pr-cycle-hook` plugin's PreToolUse hook then auto-appends ` && gh pr merge --squash --admin --delete-branch` (+ worktree/branch cleanup when run from a linked worktree) — one command does create → merge → clean. Refuses on main/master. Pass extra gh flags as args. Command half of the pr-cycle split (routing hook = `pr-cycle-hook`).

plugins.name: pr-cycle-hook
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) PR full-cycle router for `gh pr create`, in hexa-lang (`_pr_cycle.hexa`, via `hexa run`). Quote-aware tokenizer scans for the `gh pr create` 3-gram anywhere — catches bare and chained (`git push && gh pr create …`) forms. When matched (not draft / not already merging), rewrites via the `updatedInput` field (emitted with `permissionDecision: allow` in the same hookSpecificOutput — REQUIRED or the rewrite is ignored) to append ` && gh pr merge --squash --admin --delete-branch` and, IF cwd is a linked worktree, ` && (cd <main-wt> && git worktree remove <this-wt> --force; git branch -D <branch>)` — atomic create → merge → worktree+branch cleanup. Cross-repo `--repo X` routes the merge to X, skips local cleanup. Enforces commons @D g47. Skip: `--draft` · already contains `gh pr merge`. Hook half of the pr-cycle split. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: tape-lint
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Edit|Write) deny for `.tape` edits, implemented in hexa-lang (`_tape_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run` — no Python, no shell shim). Four checks, all diff-aware (pre-existing violations grandfathered, only newly-introduced or worsened items block). (1) fields rule — `@D` blocks accept only `do` / `dont`; new `why` · `tool` · `note` · `ref` · `ex` · ... refused. Fires on any `*.tape` path. (2) length cap — `do` / `dont` value > 100 chars refused (split overflow). commons.tape + project.tape only. (3) authoring-language — when `sidecar prefs` `code` axis is `english`, lines newly introducing non-Latin scripts (Hangul · CJK · Hiragana · Katakana) are refused. commons.tape + project.tape only. (4) @I siblings — project.tape's @I block must carry a `siblings` field; dropping it or adding a fresh @I without it is refused (cross-repo network SSOT). project.tape only. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pool-route
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) pool auto-router + SessionStart routing-log snapshot, implemented in hexa-lang (`_pool_route.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). When the `pool` CLI's roster (`~/.pool/pool.json`) has ≥1 enabled host and a Bash command matches the heavy classifier (make · cargo · go build · swift build · docker build · pytest · nvidia-smi · train · hexa kick/drill/loop/cc/run/build · aprime_cc · `find $HOME/core/anima` big-tree scan · …) or a root-needing command, it is rewritten via the hook `updatedInput` field (emitted with `permissionDecision: allow` in the same hookSpecificOutput) to run on a pool host over ssh — transparent dispatch, not a suggestion. Routing is EXPLICIT-classifier only: the load-escalation gate (promote any non-trivial cmd to heavy on high summed non-claude CPU) is absent because on a multi-core Mac that threshold trips constantly and ships local-bound git/hexa-run/smoke commands to Linux hosts where their paths don't exist. Local-bound guard (extended 0.6.9): `git`/`gh` commands, any `/Users/`·`/home/` absolute-path literal, and any `~/.`·`$HOME/.` home-dotstate reference always run local — dotstate forms (`~/.zsh_history`·`~/.hexa-cache`·`~/.pool`·`~/.sidecar`·…) are per-host hidden state, not user-synced, so routing them reads the WRONG host's history/cache; `~/core/...` synced workdirs still route. Sidecar plugin pin (0.6.8): cmds carrying `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` or `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA` literals always run local — they resolve via the dispatching bash to the workstation's plugin cache, which only exists on the local Mac; routing them fails (cache absent on the pool host). Trusted surface (every sidecar slash command writes its dispatch via `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` per @D s3), so no sign gate. Local-bound sign gate: within the `/Users/`·`/home/` exemption, HEAVY invocations (hexa CLI any verb · direct `hexa.real` · python/python3/py · gcc/g++/clang/clang++/cc/c++ · `bash`/`sh <script>`) require a fresh `local` sign-off token — the canonical mac fork-storm trigger; USER mints with `! sidecar sign local` in the TUI prompt. Bare absolute-path reads (ls/cat/grep/find/...) stay free — the gate narrows to interpreters/compilers only. macOS pool-host enable (0.6.6 — supersedes 0.6.1 Zero-macOS-offload): macOS pool hosts (e.g. `mini` with `os: macos`) are now auto-dispatch candidates for macOS-only commands (swift build · xcodebuild · codesign · …); general-heavy round-robin still excludes macOS hosts so generic build/test fan-out stays off the workstation. Heavy-classified commands MUST use pool resources: the hook iterates every eligible host through a preflight (workdir present + the command's toolchain actually runnable - `command -v hexa` + `test -x build/hexa_interp` + `hexa <verb> --help` for hexa, `command -v <compiler>` for C/C++, 0.6.13), routes to the first that passes, and if ALL fail emits a `permissionDecision: deny` with a per-host failure breakdown — never silently falls back to local. `hexa kick`/`drill`/`loop`/`cc` get `HEXA_LANG=$HOME/core/hexa-lang`; other hexa commands get `HEXA_LANG=$PWD`. PATH + `HEXA_MODULE_LOADER` env passthrough for bare ssh shells. Each routing decision appends one JSON line to `~/.pool/route-log.jsonl` (capped at 100); SessionStart reads the last 5 back as additionalContext. Cloud pin (0.6.10): `hexa cloud *` always runs local — it is ITSELF a Mac-local-only remote-dispatch tool, and its post-`--` remote argv (nvidia-smi · train.log · make) tripped the classifier, non-deterministically shipping `hexa cloud exec <pod> -- ...` to a Linux host with no `cloud` subcommand; double-routing a dispatcher is incoherent. Worktree→canonical-root fallback (0.6.10): a heavy command in a git LINKED worktree outside $HOME (e.g. /tmp/wt-x) previously DENIED; it now resolves the MAIN checkout via `git worktree list --porcelain` and mirrors that root when it is under $HOME (a synced ~/core workdir). Runs ONLY in the previously-denying branch, so it cannot regress a routing command. C/C++ compiler routing (0.6.11): the C/C++ compiler drivers (gcc / g++ / clang / clang++ / cc / c++) join the heavy classifier - a C/C++ compile is heavy local compute, same workstation-protection rationale as make/cmake/cargo; they carry no macOS-capability marker, so they round-robin across Linux pool hosts only (the Mac stays the workstation, per @D s12). Absolute-path compiler sign gate (0.6.12): the C/C++ compiler drivers also join the local-bound sign gate within the absolute-path exemption - an absolute-path compile (e.g. /usr/bin/gcc on a /Users path) is the same mac fork-storm trigger as a heavy interpreter, so it now needs a fresh local sign-off token (! sidecar sign local) like hexa/python/sh; relative-path compiles in synced workdirs still route to the pool. First-token basename match keeps it precise. Toolchain preflight + akida exclusion (0.6.13): (a) pi5-akida is excluded from the auto-dispatch candidate set entirely (@D s8 - anima-only; reached only via explicit `pool on pi5-akida`). (b) the host preflight now verifies the command's actual toolchain, not just the workdir - every hexa verb probes command -v hexa + test -x ~/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp + hexa <verb> --help, and a C/C++ compile probes command -v <compiler>; a host that fails is SKIPPED so round-robin moves on instead of routing-then-failing (the ubu-1 broken-hexa trap). Known limit (@D d6): a binary that passes --help but segfaults at runtime is not caught. mini-equal toggle (0.7.0): `pool mini-equal on` (writes ~/.pool/mini-equal = "on") opts macOS pool hosts (mini) INTO the OS-agnostic general-heavy round-robin as equal workers; is_linux commands still route Linux-only (the toggle only redistributes portable work). Default OFF preserves zero-macOS-offload (@D s12). Not an in-guard opt-out (@D s11) — it opens routing to an idle host, does not disable a guard. Single local-execution gate (0.7.2): a fresh `local` sign token (`! sidecar sign local`, 5min) forces EVERY command local — the one deliberate, user-minted 'run here, do NOT route' path, checked at the top before classification. When the router mis-classifies a local-bound command (diagnostic `ps`/`awk`, a local-fs fix) and ships it to a peer host, the user signs ONCE and routing is suppressed for the window. Subsumes the abs-path-heavy sign gate (signed → top-level returns first; unsigned → that gate still bites). A sanctioned time-boxed user-minted token, not an env-var opt-out (@D s11). Env-prefix + wrapper hardening (0.7.3): `_local_heavy_interp` now strips POSIX env-var prefix (`POOL_DISABLE=1`/`SIDECAR_POOL_DISABLE=1`/`FOO=bar`/…, any IDENT=val — parser correctness, not specific-var recognition) AND known leading-position wrappers (`env` · `exec` · `nice` · `timeout` · `command` · `sudo` · `nohup` · `stdbuf` · `ionice`, with per-wrapper option-arg awareness) AND a leading backslash escape (`\hexa`) before reading the real verb — pre-0.7.3 any env-var prefix OR any of those wrappers silently ALLOW'd abs-path heavy invocations, breaking sign-local's single-gate integrity. `eval` quoted-string args remain out of scope (require a real shell parser). Node-package installer pin (0.7.4): npm/pnpm/yarn run LOCAL (cwd-dependent, removed from heavy_words + added to local-bound). Routing-rate baseline counters (0.7.5 — POOL-OFFLOAD m1): every meaningful classifier verdict increments one of 4 counters in ~/.pool/route-counters.tally (routed · local_bound · light_allowed · heavy_failed) so /check reports the % offloaded vs local. Non-classifier exits NOT counted. Race-tolerant. Media tools (0.7.9 — POOL-OFFLOAD m4): heavy_words +2 (ffmpeg/magick) + abs-path first-token sign-gate +4 (ffmpeg/magick/convert/sox · convert/sox English-word substring-collide → first-token-only). JVM compilers + sbt (0.7.8 — POOL-OFFLOAD m3): heavy_words +3 (javac/kotlinc/scalac) + heavy_pairs +4 (java -jar/sbt compile/sbt test/sbt run · pair-only for short names) + abs-path first-token sign-gate +5 (java/javac/kotlinc/scalac/sbt). Node ecosystem (0.7.7 — POOL-OFFLOAD m2): heavy_words +4 (node/deno/bun/ts-node) + abs-path first-token sign-gate adds same +tsx (tsx excluded from heavy_words due to .tsx file-ext collision); mirrors python/hexa fork-storm protection. Heavy hexa verb sweep (0.7.6 — POOL-OFFLOAD m6): heavy_pairs +19 verbs — toolchain (hexa test · hexa bench) · math verifiers (hexa absolute · hexa meta-closure) · math discovery (hexa smash · hexa free · hexa hyperarithmetic) · discovery extras (hexa chain) · 11 drill variants (omega · surge · dream · swarm · reign · molt · wake · forge · canon · debate · revive) — all fork-storm class per the documented hexa 80+ verb catalog. Light verbs (atlas read/lookup · 29 annotation analyzers · 16 HTTP data bridges · lattice verify · gpu lint/disasm) excluded; 3-token verbs (hexa gpu fire · sim-universe · qmirror · atlas register --from-drill) deferred until richer classifier. OS-capability filter + round-robin load balance. NO guard opt-out by design.

plugins.name: limit-guard
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Task) hook, implemented in hexa-lang (`_limit_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`) — detects a session/usage-limit signal in a subagent result, AUTO-SNAPSHOTS uncommitted work non-destructively (`git diff HEAD` → `.claude/limit-snapshots/<ts>.patch` + status sidecar; no commit/push/stash/index mutation — shared-worktree safe; recover via `git apply`), then injects a non-blocking checkpoint directive: report progress (committed SHAs vs uncommitted), commit + push uncommitted work, write a `.claude/RESUME.md` resume manifest, stop parallel fan-out and switch to sequential commit-per-unit work. Signal match is case-insensitive (0.1.4). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: question
plugins.description: Quick side-question alias for Claude Code's built-in `/btw`. Two trigger commands: `/question <text>` (primary) and `/q <text>` (2-character alias). Same semantics as /btw — answer briefly (1-3 sentences), then resume the main work. Plugin command (markdown only, no script). Triggers: /question · /q · quick question · 잠깐 · 사이드 질문.

plugins.name: check
plugins.description: Task dashboard skill — /check prints current-state status across surfaces in one shot: `<UPPERCASE>.log.md` checkbox tasks (open vs done count + first 10 open items per log), `gh pr list` open PRs in this repo, `git status -sb` (uncommitted / ahead / behind), `git log --oneline -5` (recent merges). Read-only; no side effects. Triggers: /check · 상태 · 체크 · 현황 · status check · dashboard.

plugins.name: end
plugins.description: Session closure safety check — /end prints a single-shot dashboard of dangling residue in the current repo (uncommitted · unpushed · stash · open PRs by `@me` · marketplace.json ↔ plugin.json version drift · merged-but-undeleted local branches · linked git worktrees) with per-item ✓/⚠/○ marks + recommended actions, and a closing ✅ `100% closure — safe to end` verdict only when every line is green. Read-only — no commit / push / branch delete / worktree remove. Pairs with /ship (the actor) — /end is the verifier. Triggers: /end · 끝 · 마무리 · 종료 · 클로저 · close out · wrap up · 100% closure.

plugins.name: cycle
plugins.description: Autonomous work-loop driver. The WHOLE family auto-continues to DEPLETION — each round self-feeds from open milestones (else auto-seeds from `## deferred`), self-continues via ScheduleWakeup, and terminates only when open milestones = 0 AND deferred empty AND no other signal (per-round cap throttles WIDTH, auto-continue provides DEPTH; user can interrupt any round). 0.7.1 adds throttle-resilience: every fan-out Agent prompt is auto-injected with a CHECKPOINT-COMMIT-per-milestone clause + a rate-limit resume clause so 'Server is temporarily limiting requests' deaths lose no work and the parent can re-fire on the same worktree branch. 0.7.2 adds resource-contention: Stage-3 plan-table gains a `resource` column declaring per-item exclusive resource (e.g. `GPU:<host>-timed`), and Stage-4 serializes items sharing an exclusive resource (parallel timed fires on one GPU poison cuEvent walls — observed in the hexa-lang GPU campaign). 0.7.3 extends dup-race precheck with a stale-milestone scan: Stage-2 now ALSO scans open `[ ]` milestone labels (falsifier IDs, RFC IDs, slugs) against merged PRs + git log, marks `SKIP STALE` on hits and surfaces a flip suggestion (no auto-write) — catches the GPU.md §2 pattern of resolved milestones that nobody flipped. 0.7.4 adds cross-cutting principle injection: Stage-4 scans MEMORY.md for cross-cutting feedback memories (markers: `ALL 도메인`/`모든 도메인`/`cross-cutting`/`cross-domain`/frontmatter `cross_cutting: true`) and auto-injects up to 3 (≤300 chars each) into every fan-out Agent prompt so principles like `feedback-closure-is-physical-limit` (perf=roofline %) shape the agent's verdict framing — catches the round-7 pattern where the agent didn't report roofline % because the principle wasn't visible. 0.7.5 adds pre-fan-out worktree-leak sweep: between Stage 3 and Stage 4, auto-removes `/tmp/wt-*` linked worktrees whose branch is merged-into-main OR is stale (>1h with no recent commits) AND matches a fan-out naming convention — eliminates the manual `git worktree remove` + `git branch -D` ceremony before every re-fire after a dead/throttled agent. Safe-scoped to `/tmp` only, never `--force`, never touches user-managed worktrees. 0.7.7 adds SSOT-freshness + perpetual-domain awareness: Stage 0 verifies the active `<NAME>.md` is the LIVE SSOT (fail-open git probe — warns if untracked OR behind origin/main; catches the anima LIFE stale-shadow that wrongly read as depleted) and detects a perpetual `@goal` marker (`종료 조건 없음`/`perpetual`/`open horizon`/…); for a perpetual domain the depletion branch NEVER emits a terminal `✅ … terminates` — it first re-seeds from declared `## 영구 축`/`perpetual axes` then emits a ♾️ lane-pause (per feedback-closure-is-physical-limit). 0.8.0 fixes the autonomous-loop per-round shape + adds THE LINTER: 3 new @D - oversized_split (>=2 consecutive throttle deaths on ONE milestone => split into independently-landable sub-slices, never re-fire whole; evidence VERIFY-KIT V5 IIT engine died twice at 122/107 tool-uses fired whole, split into V5.1 promote/V5.2 wire/V5.3 calibrate each landing alone), handoff_debt_ledger (cross-repo handoff INBOX edits left uncommitted in a dirty target tree = tracked debt, cleared via isolated worktree off the target origin/main, never force-committed; evidence ARXIV A2/A3/A4 filed anima/demiurge/phanes INBOX as working-copy edits cleared by A6), round_lint (THE LINTER - each loop round emits a `round-lint: N/10 OK` line self-auditing the 10-point contract: resource-partition, checkpoint-clause, dup-race precheck, leak-sweep, <=cap, handoff-debt, oversized-split, honest-tier+progress, SSOT-freshness, perpetual-no-terminal). cycle-loop/cycle-full-loop now FIX the per-round shape: result-report (status glyph + PR# + honest verify tier + 7-element easy + progress bar) -> debt/split tracking -> round-lint line -> loop tail. Four commands: /cycle — next-list (enumerate active-domain open milestones; if empty, auto-seed ≤N (default 3) — PRIMARY signal = the domain's OWN `## deferred` section (promote next batch into the milestone board + drain from deferred so the domain self-feeds its declared backlog), then user hint · prior-turn /gap shortlist · log-tail open thread — via `/domain milestone <text>` per item, then re-enumerate; no-signal case = zero milestones + empty deferred + no signal stops with steer-options) → dup-race precheck (3-signal grep: INBOX.log.md entry status + merged PR + git log) → parallel-plan table → fan-out (one background Agent per item, same message · auto-injected with throttle-resilience clauses) → auto-continue to depletion (depletion-driving by default, not single-round-then-stop). /cycle-full — one-time phase-0 depletion brainstorm (rounds until no novel ideas, cap 8) on $ARGUMENTS, then the same precheck → plan-table → fan-out → auto-continue tail. /cycle-loop — bare /cycle handed to the `loop` skill for explicit continuous intent. /cycle-full-loop — /cycle-full once then /loop /cycle, draining to the same depletion condition. All four share the cap + 5-stage structure + plan-table + dup-race precheck + throttle-resilience + leak guardrails. Distinct from all-bg-go (reactive fan-out of PRIOR-turn branches; /cycle self-generates the next batch each round).

plugins.name: pool
plugins.description: /pool <args> — wraps the `pool` CLI (host roster + remote exec — minimal, zero deps). Verbs: list · add <host> · on <host> <cmd> · status · install tailscale · rm <host>. NL trigger via skill. Pairs with commons g9 + sidecar's pool-route hook.

plugins.name: cloud
plugins.description: /cloud <args> — wraps `hexa cloud` (runpod / vast.ai dispatch · canonical subcommand form, structured argv — never raw runpodctl/vastai/ssh/scp/curl per commons g8, hard-enforced by the cloud-guard hook). Subverbs: preflight · run · nohup · poll · tail · copy-to · copy-from. preflight = closed-form GPU mem-budget check (refuses out-of-budget specs before pod spinup · no LLM · $0). tail = live remote-log stream → Monitor bridge (ssh tail -F | sed -u · terminal-exit on a crash-aware default · commons g57). NL trigger via skill. 0.3.5 — atomic `hexa cloud fire <host> [--log <path>] -- <argv>` (auto-log `/tmp/cloud-<unix_ts>.log`) + `__MONITOR_HANDLE__={…}` JSON-line contract are SHIPPED upstream (hexa-lang PR #1306 + #1309); SKILL.md documents the live one-shot workflow (fire → grep handle → tail attach) instead of the pre-ship 3-step manual handoff. Pairs with commons g8 + g12 + g57.

plugins.name: hexa-help
plugins.description: /hexa-help [verb] — wraps `hexa --help` (no arg, top-level catalog) or `hexa <verb> --help` (verb-specific). Per commons g7. NL trigger via skill.

plugins.name: secret
plugins.description: /secret <args> — wraps the `secret` CLI (macOS Keychain-backed credentials, dancinlab/secret 0.4.0, dual-channel sync). Verbs: get · set · rotate · check · delete · list · service · init [icloud|github <url>] · backup [enable <url>|disable|status] · sync · migrate. Two independent sync channels (iCloud Drive primary + optional private GitHub mirror) push the same encrypted blob — master password is the only decryption secret. Auto-push ON by default once `backup enable` runs (opt out via SECRET_BACKUP_AUTO=0). High-value protection on set (BIP39 wordlist-validated · xprv/WIF/64-hex auto-detect → refuse without --allow-mnemonic + stdin/tty). rotate emits sentinel only.

plugins.name: quota
plugins.description: /quota:quota <args> — Claude account 5h/7d usage limits + multi-account registry + live credential swap + per-account nicknames. Single-dispatch picker entry; verb routed via $ARGUMENTS. Verbs: list (= status = bare · UNIFIED all-accounts table in chat, 6-col English header: Nickname · Email · Session Used · Session Reset · Weekly Used · Weekly Reset, ▓░ 5-slot bar + humanized time-to-reset; rows sorted by nickname (else email), case-insensitive; the active account is ★ + fetched LIVE (45s cache), every other account shows its last per-account cache, `—` = never fetched) · all (same table, every account fetched LIVE in one shot, in PARALLEL — the easy toggle to 'refresh everything') · add [<nick>] (snapshot account + capture credentials into backup store · optional nickname like claude1) · nick <ref> [<nick>] (set or clear a nickname for an existing account; bare nick arg = clear) · switch <ref> (live cred swap, verified + rollback on failure) · remove <ref> (rm · delete · del — drop registry row + backup creds, leaves live ~/.claude.json untouched) · refresh [<ref>] (re-fetch usage into per-account cache; renews expired tokens via refreshToken) · autoadd (silent SessionStart-hook path — registers the active account iff not yet in the registry; driven by the quota-autoadd hook plugin so a fresh `claude /login` self-registers next session) · help. The view also auto-registers the live account if untracked (autoadd parity). <ref> = nickname (e.g. claude1) · email · numeric index — lookup order index→nick→email. Live fetch via OAuth usage endpoint (api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage) + auto refresh-token grant (platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token); 45s per-account cache. Per-account OAuth blob backup in macOS keychain svc 'sidecar-quota' / linux ~/.sidecar/quota/creds/<email>.json (0600 + atomic write). own-accounts-serial only — never a multi-tenant router (ToS red-line). Agent SDK credit (dual-bucket since 2026-06-15) is documented as future direction; current release tracks subscription 5h/7d only. The bare/list view renders from cache instantly then spawns a detached background stale-while-revalidate refresh of stale non-active accounts (cache > 300s, with backup creds) so the NEXT view trends fresh without an explicit `all` — the view never blocks on the network; a dead/expired backup token is skipped for a 1800s cooldown (shows `—` until `refresh <ref>` renews it); the active account keeps a 45s inline live refresh. Both `all` and the background cycle fetch their account set in parallel (`_parallel_refresh` — token-prep sequential, usage GETs concurrent). ⚠ uses non-public Anthropic OAuth endpoints — may break if rotated.

plugins.name: quota-autoadd
plugins.description: SessionStart hook — silently auto-registers the active Claude account into the quota registry when a fresh `claude /login` introduces an account not yet tracked. Invokes the quota skill's `_quota.hexa autoadd` (idempotent · always exit 0 · prints one line only when a NEW account lands; already-registered sessions are fully silent). The add path also captures the OAuth blob into the backup store so `/quota:quota switch` works for the new account immediately. Pairs with the `quota` skill — quota-autoadd is the default-on registration layer (manual `/quota:quota add` remains). Resolves the quota binary via $HOME/.claude/plugins/cache/sidecar/quota/<version>/bin/_quota.hexa (portable; $HOME-based; picks the latest cached version by semver `sort -V`, not mtime). NO opt-out by design (commons g11).

plugins.name: kick
plugins.description: /kick <natural-language seed> — runs `hexa kick --seed "<seed>"` (hexa-lang gap-breakthrough / discovery engine, aliased to `hexa drill`). All args join into the seed. NL trigger via skill (`kick this`, `돌파해줘`, `discover for`). Pairs with commons g6.

plugins.name: verify
plugins.description: /verify <args> — runs `hexa verify "$@"` (cross-project tier rubric, TECS-L-aligned). Forms: <id> (atlas atom), --expr <fn> <n> <v> (numerical), --fence "<claim>" (honest ⚪), rubric / list. Returns 🔵🟢🟡🟠🔴⚪ tier badge VERBATIM. NL trigger via skill (`verify this`, `확인해`, `검증해`, `맞아?`). Pairs with commons g5.

plugins.name: atlas
plugins.description:
/atlas — wraps `hexa atlas` (atlas SSOT surface). SSOT = `compiler/atlas/embedded.gen.hexa` (binary-builtin atlas, ~16088 nodes), served at runtime by static_atlas via TEXT-parse of embedded.gen.hexa. Read verbs: hash · stats [--audit [--scope=rodata|overlay|merged] [--format=json]] · lookup [K] / lookup --prefix=

(K ∈ P|C|L|E|F|R|S|X|Q) · dump [K] [--json]. Write verb (ONE): register --from-verify (or 2-op) · register --from-drill --seed "" [--rounds N] [--engine mk9|mk10] — runs the verb in-process and on 🔵/🟢 folds the verified AtlasNode DIRECTLY into compiler/atlas/embedded.gen.hexa, visible to the next lookup with NO rebuild, NO staging shard, NO intermediate file (commit embedded.gen.hexa with normal git to share). Export verb (NEW): export [--out PATH] — writes the live atlas out as a portable .n6 file (default n6/atlas.n6, an export artifact only, NOT the runtime SSOT). NL trigger via skill (`atlas lookup`, `atlas stats`, `아틀라스 조회`, `아틀라스 등록`, `register from drill`, `atlas absorb`).

plugins.name: domain
plugins.description: Maintain UPPERCASE <NAME>.md (snapshot = final-goal milestone checkboxes) + sister <NAME>.log.md (append-only step log) at project root — auto-scaffolds both, defaults NAME to uppercase basename of git root. Verbs: /domain <NAME> (SELECT the session's active domain → later verbs default to it + show a final-goal progress bar `▓▓▓░░ NN% · done/total` from the snapshot's checkboxes) · /domain (show active) · /domain list (alias ls — repo-wide index; with a DOMAINS.tape roster it is AUTHORITATIVE: tables each registered domain ★ = active · @goal · progress · location, progress/@goal stay DERIVED so the checked-in roster holds only NAME→path and never churns; flags ghosts + unregistered; no roster → legacy disk-scan) · /domain list --sync (reconcile DOMAINS.tape with disk — bootstraps the roster) · /domain <task> (append `- [x]` to log) · /domain todo <task> (`- [ ]`) · /domain done <match> (flip [ ]→[x]) · /domain title <text> (alias subtitle — OPTIONAL `@title:` display header: icon · name · alias, e.g. `🧠 IIT4 — 의식 측정자(尺)`, rendered in place of plain `active domain: NAME`; absence keeps current output, no lint warning) · /domain new <header>. Progress is final-goal-based (snapshot completion), not log-based — it doesn't drift as the log grows. Path resolution: a DOMAINS.tape roster row (`@domain NAME := "path"`) wins FIRST, so a domain can live at ANY path (e.g. `domains/RUNTIME/RUNTIME.md`); absent a roster entry it falls back to root → folder-nested → root-default-scaffold (log follows the snapshot's dir), so folder-nested meta-domains no longer regenerate an empty root scaffold each call. The folder-nested fallback only matches when the nested file's first line is the domain header `# <NAME>` (guards against false-matching an unrelated same-named file like a digest on case-insensitive filesystems). 0.8.8: /domain set now SELF-HEALS the DOMAINS.tape roster (auto-registers an on-disk-but-unregistered domain — the gap that ghosted anima's LIFE) and warns on a stale SSOT shadow (the working-tree <NAME>.md is UNTRACKED or BEHIND origin/main → progress/closure may read a stale local copy; reference_domain_init_untracked_ssot); a perpetual `@goal` (no-termination marker like `종료 조건 없음`/`perpetual`/`open horizon`) renders a ♾️ perpetual badge so the progress bar reads as frontier-depletion, NOT a 100% completion countdown (per feedback-closure-is-physical-limit).

plugins.name: gap
plugins.description: /gap — multi-axis gap exploration. 42 breakthrough-strategy lenses · 8 families (F4 + F6 each have 6 lenses; `occams-razor` lives in both — hypothesis side and design side). Bare /gap = inline-triage + deep-dive only hot families. /gap full = exhaustive 8-subagent fan-out. /gap <scope> targets the sweep. /gap list prints the catalogue. Surfaces + prioritises gaps; never fixes.

plugins.name: sidecar
plugins.description: /sidecar <args> — thin wrapper over the `sidecar` marketplace CLI (host-local, on PATH via `hx install sidecar`). Verbs — init (scaffold project.tape + CLAUDE.md symlink + LATTICE_POLICY.md) · sync (pull marketplace + refresh cache + installed_plugins.json + enable plugins + mirror commands) · mirror (refresh ~/.claude/commands/ mirrors so plugin commands are bare-invocable — `Skill("imagine")` instead of `Skill("imagine:imagine")` — auto-runs at sync tail) · shadow [plan] (drop the FORCED `plugin:command` namespace #15882 — mirror+disable every command/skill plugin, splicing SKILL.md triggers into the bare command so it stays lossless; hook/mcp kept; `plan` = dry-run; re-asserted at sync tail) · unshadow (revert) · sign (user sign-off gate). `sign <key>` mints a 5-min token at ~/.sidecar/signs/<key>.sign unlocking agent edits to a sign-gated governance SSOT file — the sign-guard hook hard-denies those edits until a fresh token exists; keys are `commons` (commons.tape) and `project` (project.tape). Bare `sign` / `sign --list` shows active tokens; `sign clear [<key>]` removes them. Multi-user enable profiles — `profile [<name>]` shows/sets the enable profile (minimal = core only · hexa = core + hexa-lang · full = everything, default · master = full + creator-only). `master [on|off|status]` mints/removes the ~/.sidecar/master marker. `enable`/`disable <plugin>` force one plugin on/off (per-plugin override), `reset <plugin>` drops the override; each re-applies the install. State in ~/.sidecar/profile + plugin-overrides.json; install.hexa enables per .claude-plugin/profiles.json tiers — plugin disable, not an in-guard opt-out (@D s11 safe). Args pass through unchanged.

plugins.name: inject
plugins.description: Immediately inject the latest sidecar commons.tape + project.tape into the CURRENT session and sync the local install for the next session. /inject (and `/ij` short alias) runs `sidecar sync` (marketplace pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch) AND prints the current commons.tape + (cwd's) project.tape so the model picks them up this turn.

plugins.name: ship
plugins.description: Atomic ship tail for sidecar plugin changes — /ship -m "<msg>" <path>... stages explicit paths (never -A/-u), credential-scans the staged diff (rpa_ · sk- · hf_ · AKIA), commits, pushes origin/<branch>, then runs `sidecar sync` (marketplace pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch). Mechanical tail of @D ship / commons g27; the agent owns version bump + surface lockstep + message FIRST. Bare /ship (no -m / no paths) is helper mode (0.3.2): lists uncommitted changes + emits a ready-to-edit `/ship -m "..." <paths>` template instead of a usage error, exits 0, still never auto-stages. NL trigger via skill (ship · 배포 · 출시).

plugins.name: bypass
plugins.description: Anti-punt — DEFAULT BEHAVIOR (auto-fires, not opt-in). Universal self-check before any move that hands control back to the user (interactive input · unauthorized destructive · external visible · explicit user-review request); if all NO, just execute. Extensible catalog of patterns: 'next user action:' blocks · 'Should I proceed?' confirmations · 'Want me to check?' deferrals · option-trees with obvious default · over-clarification · defer-by-waiting · excessive recap. Cross-project always-on enforcement in commons.tape ≥0.7.3.

plugins.name: brainstorm
plugins.description: Iterative brainstorming — given a seed, generates ideas in rounds and keeps going until depletion (no genuinely new ideas vs prior rounds; hard cap 8 rounds). For breadth over selection. Skill (natural language) + /brainstorm command.

plugins.name: easy
plugins.description: Easy (friendly) response style — 7-element pattern (icon · name · alias · plain-line · analogy · ASCII · compare). Skill (natural-language trigger) + /easy command. 5 language samples: en · ko · ja · zh · ru. /easy command body now points at $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT with a cache-resolved fallback (0.1.2) so the bare-invocable user-level mirror works.

plugins.name: easy-auto
plugins.description: SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit + PreCompact + PostCompact hook — auto-injects the `easy` (friendly) 7-element response style as additionalContext so the pattern is active from turn 0 without the user calling /easy. Reads ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/styles/easy.<lang>.md (env `EASY_LANG` override, default `ko`); falls back to easy.md (English base) when the lang variant is missing. UserPromptSubmit re-injects every turn so the style never fades; PreCompact + PostCompact survive auto-compaction. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_easy_auto.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Pairs with the `easy` skill (manual /easy trigger remains — easy-auto is the default-on layer). `EASY_LANG` only SELECTS the style language (default `ko`) — not a disable switch. NO opt-out by design (commons @D g11 / sidecar @D s11); 0.1.2 removed the `EASY_LANG=off` suppress path.

plugins.name: research
plugins.description: Research-fetch tools — `/research:arxiv <query|id>` searches the official arXiv API or fetches by id (title · authors · abstract · pdf); `/research:yt <url-or-id>` extracts YouTube caption transcript via the InnerTube ANDROID client. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_arxiv.hexa` / `_yt.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`); HTTP via curl, no API key. Error paths (network · parse · no-captions · bad-id) exit non-zero so callers can tell failure from a real result.

plugins.name: gh-stack
plugins.description: Stacked-PR workflow skill — proposes `gh stack` commands (enabled repos) or the manual `gh pr create --base previous-layer` fallback. Encodes the <200-lines-per-layer · 1-concern governance.

plugins.name: paper
plugins.description: /paper <args> — arxiv-style LaTeX paper scaffolder (v0.9 — monograph mode). Verbs: new <slug> · monograph-init <slug> [N] · fill <appendix-letter> · sample [<name>] [<dest>] · fig <size> <prompt> <out.png> · compile [dir] (xelatex × 3 + bibtex) · lint [dir] (commons @D g51 — auto paper/monograph tier) · list · help · companion {init|sync} · outline [dir] · pipeline <stage1> ... · atoms <domain> · verify-block <fn> <args...> <expected> · bib add <doi-or-arxiv> · pr-roll <repo> <since-ref> · arxiv-prep [<dir>]. v0.9 adds monograph mode: `monograph-init` scaffolds a main.tex \appendix + N (default 12) appendix/<L>_*.tex chapter stubs + companion/ (the HEXA-FUSION 58p structure); `fill` prints a per-appendix runbook; `companion sync` rebuilds the data records. lint now traverses \input'd appendix files for figure/table counts (the 58p build's #1 bug), auto-switches to a monograph tier (>=30p/>=6figs/companion when \appendix + >=6 \input files), and warns on comparison tables with no chart. The Makefile lists appendix/*.tex + figure PDFs as deps (edit triggers rebuild, no distclean); pgfplots ships in the default preamble. FOUR bundled samples: sample-nb-bcs-absorbed · sample-fusion-7gate · sample-cost-routing · sample-blue-max.

plugins.name: imagine
plugins.description: /imagine <prompt-file> <out.png> [-s size] [-b backend] [-m model] — generic AI image generator. Two backends out of the box: `fal` (queue+poll fal.ai, default — `secret get fal.api_key`, model = openai/gpt-image-2 firm-pinned default) and `openai` (sync /v1/images/generations — `secret get openai.api_key`, model = gpt-image-1). Canonical fal-style size tokens (`square_hd` / `landscape_16_9` / `portrait_16_9` / `square`) translate per-backend. Prompt always read from a file (provenance), payload JSON via mktemp (prompt never on argv). Backend pluggable — drop a new `_backends/<name>.hexa` to add one.

plugins.name: output-trim
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) stdout trimmer — when a simple single Bash command would emit >8000 chars, transparently rewrite via `updatedInput` (emitted with `permissionDecision: allow` in the same hookSpecificOutput — REQUIRED, else Claude Code ignores the rewrite and stdout is never trimmed; the 0.1.3 fix) to pipe stdout through a minimal hexa-native kernel: consecutive-line dedup ([×N] counter) + per-line truncation to 200 chars. Small outputs <8000 chars pass verbatim. Skipped on any shell metacharacter so compound pipelines / redirects / heredocs / background jobs / command substitutions and other PreToolUse(Bash) rewrite hooks compose without conflict. Exit code preserved via `set -o pipefail`. Kernel chosen by benchmark on 120 real Bash-stdout samples ≥8000 chars — Pareto-dominates the TF-IDF salience + MinHash near-dup paragraph dedup approach of the original `wilson-output-trim`. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: subagent-route
plugins.description: [POC] PreToolUse(Task|Agent) observation hook + SessionStart observation-log snapshot, implemented in hexa-lang (`_subagent_route.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). v0.1.0 is observation-only — it does NOT deny, NOT rewrite. On every Task/Agent fire it dumps `tool_input` (key list + agent_type + prompt preview + isolation) to `~/.sidecar/subagent-route.log.jsonl` (capped 100 lines, jsonl format mirroring pool-route's route-log) and emits a non-blocking `additionalContext` advisory suggesting the equivalent `ssh <host> -- claude --print --no-tty -p '<prompt>'` rewrite. SessionStart event reads the last 5 observations back as additionalContext so the model sees recent subagent fires without recon. PoC purpose — probe the actual tool_input payload shape at runtime (Claude Code docs leave Agent/Task tool_input fields under-specified vs Bash), and probe the `additionalContext` UX before a real-rewrite v0.2.0. Always exits 0; advisory only.

plugins.name: worktree-gc
plugins.description: SessionStart hook that prunes merged-but-undeleted LINKED git worktrees in the cwd repo, implemented in hexa-lang (`_worktree_gc.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Threshold gate (linked-count >= 3 OR disk avail < 20 GiB) keeps the common case (1-2 worktrees, ample disk) silent. Above threshold, fetches `gh pr list --state merged --limit 200` and prunes any linked worktree whose branch is in that merged set OR whose `origin/<branch>` ref is gone (force-removes the worktree directory and deletes the local branch). Open-PR worktrees are NEVER touched. gh unavailable / no merged PRs → short note + no action. Always exits 0 (non-blocking). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: sidecar-auto-sync
plugins.description: SessionStart hook that runs `sidecar sync` once per Claude Code session, implemented in hexa-lang (`_sidecar_auto_sync.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Closes the gap between an upstream `git push` on origin/main and the local plugin cache — no more manual `sidecar sync` between sessions. Silent on success; brief stderr advisory on failure. Skipped silently only on a real precondition: the `sidecar` binary not on PATH (fresh machine without `hx install sidecar` yet). Adds ~1-2s to session startup (network pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch). NO opt-out by design (project.tape @D s11) — no env var / config disable switch; uninstall the plugin for manual control.

plugins.name: s9-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for load-assessment commands (project.tape @D s9). Fires when a Bash command looks like a Mac load check — `uptime` (word) · `loadavg` / `vm.loadavg` (substring) · `ps -A` adjacency or `ps -Ao pcpu` · `top -l ` (macOS) / `top -b ` (Linux) · `getloadavg`. Emits non-blocking additionalContext reminding the agent that any conclusion drawn from raw load must EXCLUDE `claude` PIDs (claude sessions are exempt from local load attribution) and suggesting the canonical claude-excluded sum (`ps -Ao pcpu,comm | awk '$2 !~ /claude/ {s+=$1} END {print int(s)}'`). NEVER blocks — pure advisory. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_s9_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Ships per @D s7 (governance + enforcement together) — the @D s9 companion guard. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: step-by-step
plugins.description: /step-by-step:step-by-step (alias /step-by-step:sbs) — plan-first sequential runbook. Decomposes the task into an ordered, numbered plan, then executes every step strictly in order WITHOUT pausing between steps, narrating each step's start + ✅/⚠/❌ result. Halts only on a step failure (reports the failing step + verbatim error + un-run tail) or before a genuinely irreversible / destructive / outward-facing step (confirm-then-resume, same bar as the bypass self-check). The deliberate single-threaded counterpart to /cycle's parallel fan-out — one thread, top to bottom. Bare = the task in context; /step-by-step:step-by-step <task> targets explicitly.

plugins.name: micro-exp
plugins.description: /micro-exp [scope] — context-driven micro-experiment sweep orchestrator. Self-enumerate sweep candidates from the conversation / active domain (no manifest file — like /cycle) → rent pods (≤budget) → copy inputs → run → 🛰️ Monitor (pw.x|ph.x alive-check) → on JOB DONE harvest+down + dispatch parse Agent → atlas register on 🟢 (generic verify-delegation, folds direct to embedded.gen.hexa) → aggregate ledger at exports/sweep/<batch>/. Inverse of 1-big-run: N small verify-able experiments in parallel; atlas auto-folds; FALSIFIED is a CLOSED negative (honest, never skipped — per @D g63). 0.3.0 adds @D local_pool_adapter: when context yields a wall-measurement / structural-oracle matrix (not DFT-elph / SSCHA) AND a viable local pool host is ON (`ubu-2` for GPU walls, `mini` for arm64), switches to local-pool mode — dispatch via `pool on <host>` instead of `hexa cloud rent`, skip atlas register where wall-only, budget=`local_hours_max` — catches the hexa-lang 'no candidate matrix' false-punt on the GPU decomposition sweep. Bare = current context · arg = scope filter. Per-candidate {id, kind, inputs_dir, pseudo_dir, parser_template} inferred from domain convention; batch_id auto-derived; budget declared before dispatch. Distinct from /cycle (milestone-driven loop) and /all-bg-go (reactive single fan-out). NL trigger via skill ('/micro-exp', '스윕 던져', '수십개 동시', 'micro experiment fan-out', 'sweep these', 'verify-driven sweep').

plugins.name: stdlib-ssot-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit) + SessionStart advisory for commons @D g61 (hexa-lang stdlib = SSOT for shared code, primitives AND domain engines), in hexa-lang (invoked via `hexa run`). NON-BLOCKING (additionalContext only). On a `.hexa` edit it nudges when content fights single-SSOT: (a) abs-path import of another repo's `.hexa` (anima-locked) — promote to stdlib + import "stdlib/…"; (b) defining a `fn NAME` that already exists as a `pub fn` in hexa-lang stdlib (duplicate) — import it (best-effort grep, fail-safe). SessionStart checks the single physical stdlib root (~/core/hexa-lang/stdlib). Edits under hexa-lang/stdlib skipped. Pairs with the /stdlib skill.

plugins.name: stdlib
plugins.description: /stdlib — hexa-lang stdlib SSOT helper (commons @D g61). `check` (bare) scans the cwd repo for g61 violations: abs-path cross-repo `.hexa` imports + local fn names duplicating a hexa-lang stdlib `pub fn`. `promote <file>` prints the move-to-stdlib + thin-shim runbook (engine⊥adapter; the phi_native / IIT4 #1051 flow). Read-only scan; promote prints a runbook. Pairs with the stdlib-ssot-guard hook.
